name: Bainbridge High School
narrative:
In 2023, a $300,000 verdict was rendered against the Bainbridge Island School District following a finding of negligence in failing to protect a student from aggressive bullying and sexual harassment during the student's enrollment. A retired judge awarded the damages at the district level, and the cited records do not reflect any subsequent appeal or resolution beyond the verdict itself. [Sources: https://pcva.law/press-releases/bainbridge-island-school-district-found-negligent-bullying-case-student-autism/]

Also in 2023, a student died, and the Bainbridge Island School District reached a settlement of $1.325 million with the student's family. This settlement was reported at the district level, and the cited records do not reflect any further proceedings beyond the settlement. [Sources: https://www.kitsapdailynews.com/news/bisd-settles-suit-over-suicide-for-1-325-million/]

In 2026, the principal of Bainbridge High School was charged with allegations of driving under the influence, reckless endangerment, and driving with a suspended license following an arrest by local police. The principal was placed on leave following the arrest. Available records as of the time of reporting do not reflect a disposition beyond the placement on leave. [Sources: https://mynorthwest.com/crime-blotter/bainbridge-high-school-principal/4189047]

name: Fairhaven Middle School
narrative:
In December 2022, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools alleging failures to report sexual assault, citing "a pattern of extreme and outrageous conduct" and raising grounds including civil rights violations and negligent infliction of emotional distress. That lawsuit developed alongside a related criminal matter: in December 2023, at the district level, three district administrators resolved criminal charges for failure to report sexual assault through a deferred prosecution agreement, which required community service and education regarding Title IX. The cited records do not reflect a further resolution of the federal civil lawsuit. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2023/dec/12/criminal-charges-against-bellingham-public-schools-administrators-resolved/]

Separately, in 2025, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools alleging Title IX violations and negligence after reporting repeated sexual harassment and physical assault by another student to administrators, both in person and through text messages. Available records as of the time of generation show no reported resolution of that lawsuit. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2025/mar/24/district-sued-family-of-student-who-was-sexually-harassed-calls-response-catastrophically-broken/]

Also at the district level, in 2026, Bellingham Public Schools admitted liability for negligence in a lawsuit brought by a family after a student was sexually assaulted on a school bus in spring 2023. Sources reported that the individual responsible had a documented history of assaulting other students on the bus. The cited records do not reflect a final settlement amount or other resolution beyond the admission of liability. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2026/feb/05/bellingham-public-schools-admits-liability-in-school-bus-sexual-assault-case/]

name: Sehome High School
narrative:
At the district level, in December 2022, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools alleging a failure to report sexual assault and citing "a pattern of extreme and outrageous conduct," with grounds including civil rights violations and negligent infliction of emotional distress. Related to this matter, in December 2023, three district administrators resolved criminal charges for failure to report sexual assault through a deferred prosecution agreement, which required community service and education regarding Title IX. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2023/dec/12/criminal-charges-against-bellingham-public-schools-administrators-resolved/]

Separate district-level legal actions also came to light in subsequent years. In 2025, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools alleging Title IX violations and negligence after reporting repeated sexual harassment and physical assault by a fellow student to administrators — both in person and through text messages. The cited records do not reflect a resolution of that lawsuit. In 2026, Bellingham Public Schools admitted liability for negligence in a lawsuit brought by a family whose child was sexually assaulted on a school bus in spring 2023; the district acknowledged that the perpetrator had a documented history of assaulting other students on the bus. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2025/mar/24/district-sued-family-of-student-who-was-sexually-harassed-calls-response-catastrophically-broken/; https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2026/feb/05/bellingham-public-schools-admits-liability-in-school-bus-sexual-assault-case/]

At Sehome High School specifically, in an undated report, an investigation by Bellingham Public Schools found serious, confirmed hazing involving members of the school's football team that violated district harassment, intimidation, and bullying policies. The varsity football team forfeited a game as a result, and the principal addressed the incident in a message to families. [Sources: https://www.king5.com/article/news/local/bellingham/bellingham-sehome-high-school-football-team-hazing/281-fa900f4f-aaa8-4446-a4eb-d3b345c1a1e6]

name: Squalicum High School
narrative:
In December 2022, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools, alleging that the district failed to report allegations of sexual assault and citing "a pattern of extreme and outrageous conduct," with grounds including civil rights violations and negligent infliction of emotional distress. At the district level, that matter was connected to a broader pattern of institutional conduct: in December 2023, three district administrators resolved criminal charges for failure to report sexual assault through a deferred prosecution agreement, which required community service and education regarding Title IX. The cited records do not reflect a resolution of the federal lawsuit. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2023/dec/12/criminal-charges-against-bellingham-public-schools-administrators-resolved/]

Separately, in 2025, a student filed a federal lawsuit against Bellingham Public Schools at the district level, alleging Title IX violations and negligence after the student reported repeated sexual harassment and physical assault by a fellow student to administrators, both in person and through text messages. The cited records do not reflect a resolution of that lawsuit. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2025/mar/24/district-sued-family-of-student-who-was-sexually-harassed-calls-response-catastrophically-broken/]

Also involving district-level liability, Bellingham Public Schools admitted negligence in a lawsuit stemming from a sexual assault that occurred on a school bus in spring 2023. According to available records, the individual responsible had a documented history of assaulting other students on the bus. The district's admission of liability was reported in 2026. [Sources: https://www.cascadiadaily.com/2026/feb/05/bellingham-public-schools-admits-liability-in-school-bus-sexual-assault-case/]

name: Juanita High School
narrative:
In 2025, at the district level, a federal investigation by the U.S. Office of Civil Rights was underway following a legal complaint filed by the legal team for a nonprofit organization. The complaint alleged that the district violated the civil rights of its Jewish students by ignoring and enabling antisemitism. As reported in 2025, the investigation was underway; available records as of the date of this briefing do not reflect a resolution. [Sources: https://www.king5.com/article/news/education/federal-investigation-lake-washington-school-district-anti-semitism/281-acbcca35-b8bf-4c7c-bc4c-478bb3d5d984]

Also at the district level, a lawsuit alleged that the district failed to follow its own mandated bullying investigation procedures after a student experienced a health episode and reported bullying. The district was required under its own policies to begin bullying investigations within two days and complete them within ten days; the suit alleged those timelines were not met. After spending nearly $1 million in taxpayer funds to contest the case — despite the family having offered to settle for $15,000 four years earlier — the district agreed in 2022 to a settlement of $313,500. [Sources: https://albertlawpllc.com/school-bullying-and-%C2%A7504-violations-settlement-reached/]

Separately, a lawsuit filed in January 2024 in King County Superior Court alleged that the district, which had agreed to pay hundreds of thousands of dollars to place a student at an out-of-state boarding school, failed to monitor the student's condition, achievement, or safety while enrolled there. According to the complaint, staff at the placement did not follow the student's behavioral plan, and the alleged neglect resulted in a serious eye injury that permanently impaired the student's vision. The cited records do not reflect a resolution of that litigation. [Sources: https://www.propublica.org/article/shrub-oak-international-school-lawsuit-washington-state-autism]

In 2015, several students at Juanita High School were charged with attempted rape of a student. An investigation revealed that school officials — including those in leadership and athletics administration roles — discouraged police involvement and failed to notify the district's Risk and Safety Management office as required. Available records do not reflect a final disposition of all institutional accountability matters arising from those findings. [Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Juanita_High_School]

name: Phantom Lake Elementary
narrative:
In 2024, the principal of Phantom Lake Elementary was placed on administrative leave after making comments about a swastika found on the playground that parents deemed inappropriate, with the principal referencing multiple perspectives on the symbol. The Bellevue School District launched an investigation at that time. In January 2026, the same principal was placed on administrative leave following a separate incident in which the principal told 911 dispatchers that a school aide experiencing a seizure was a cocaine user based on hearsay. An interim principal was serving in the role during that period. Available records do not reflect a disposition for either matter. [Sources: https://komonews.com/news/local/principal-bellevue-school-district-phantom-lake-elementary-swastika-buddhism-hinduism-cultural-awareness-education-parents-students-safety-statement-emails-investigation; https://www.kuow.org/stories/bellevue-principal-on-leave-after-claiming-a-school-aide-s-seizure-stemmed-from-cocaine-use]

At the district level, in November 2017, students and a parent filed a lawsuit against the Bellevue School District alleging negligence in the district's refusal to protect students from harassment, intimidation, bullying, and discrimination by investigators appointed to review alleged violations by a high school football program. Separately, as reported in 2017, the U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights was pursuing an investigation into the district and the Washington Interscholastic Activities Association based on concerns about the treatment of students and racial targeting by investigators. The cited records do not reflect a resolution for either matter. [Sources: https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/students-file-lawsuit-against-bellevue-school-district-and-the-washington-interscholastic-activities-association-for-failure-to-protect-them-from-harassment-intimidation-and-bullying-according-to-jones-law-office-300549391.html]

Also at the district level, in 2018, the Bellevue School District faced a retrial on a bullying lawsuit originally filed in 2014. The family alleged the district was negligent and breached its duty to protect a student who became the subject of bullying, sexual harassment, and assault following the student's report of a sexual assault incident on a school bus in December 2010. The cited records do not reflect a resolution for that retrial. [Sources: https://www.bellevuereporter.com/news/retrial-for-bullying-lawsuit-against-bellevue-school-district-set-for-april-30/]

In June 2022, a student filed a lawsuit and a $3 million notice of claim against the Bellevue School District, alleging the district abandoned the student after the student reported being falsely accused of dating violence by another student. Available records as of the time of this briefing's generation do not reflect a resolution. [Sources: https://komonews.com/news/local/bellevue-school-district-hit-with-lawsuit-by-student-over-ex-gf-accusations]

Regarding district finances, in 2025, the Bellevue School District fell under financial oversight from the Washington State Office of Superintendent of Public Instruction after the School Board passed a resolution for binding conditions with that office, making the district the eighth in Washington state reported to require assistance balancing its budget. [Sources: https://komonews.com/news/local/bellevue-school-district-budget-oversight-washington-state-office-superintendent-public-instruction-funding-ospi]
